title: Production AI
description: Ship reliable AI products with architecture, monitoring, and safe operations.

curatedResources.title: AI Engineering Hub
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use as inspiration for shipping and hardening AI applications.

curatedResources.title: Building Effective Agents
curatedResources.placement: Use for robust control logic before moving to production automations.
curatedResources.why: Strengthens agent-style reasoning, boundaries, and safe failure behavior for production AI workflows.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Review when designing autonomous or agent-like production automations.

curatedResources.title: MCP Practical Guide
curatedResources.placement: Use during tool and external integration work.
curatedResources.why: Practical reference for connecting AI systems to tools, APIs, and data planes.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use before implementing external integrations in production flows.

curatedResources.title: AI Engineering
curatedResources.placement: Read for architecture and operations perspective.
curatedResources.why: Covers production realities and design tradeoffs for real AI systems.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use while reviewing module-level architecture and deployment decisions.

outcomes: Design resilient AI system architecture for production traffic.
Build API and queue patterns that survive spikes.
Set up evaluation and observability for model-driven features.
Control cost and security for long-running AI products.
Plan secure deployment and incident response.

lessons.title: AI System Architecture
lessons.description: Define service boundaries, model interfaces, and data flow.
checklist: I can draw a minimal production architecture.
I can separate sync and async paths.
I can explain where controls sit.
questions: What belongs in orchestration versus model-facing services?
Where is observability inserted first?

lessons.title: APIs
lessons.description: Build API contracts that keep AI features stable and testable.
checklist: I can version API contracts.
I can secure endpoint boundaries.
I can define timeout and error contracts.
questions: How do you keep model changes from breaking clients?
What status and error patterns build resilient API consumers?

curatedResources.title: MCP Practical Guide
curatedResources.placement: Read before wiring external tool integrations into APIs.
curatedResources.why: Practical patterns for safe tool and API orchestration.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use while designing external call boundaries and request contracts.

curatedResources.title: AI Engineering
curatedResources.placement: Use before finalizing API and orchestration contracts.
curatedResources.why: Provides practical architecture tradeoffs for AI service boundaries.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use to inform endpoint design and fallback policy choices.

lessons.title: Queues
lessons.description: Use queues for asynchronous workflows and reliable retries.
checklist: I can pick queue patterns for long tasks.
I can set retry and dead-letter policies.
I can preserve idempotency.
questions: Which AI tasks should never be purely synchronous?
How do you prevent duplicate processing?

lessons.title: Evaluation
lessons.description: Create ongoing checks for quality, drift, and regressions.
checklist: I can design regression test sets.
I can track quality trends over time.
I can alert on significant drift.
questions: What is the minimum useful evaluation cadence?
How do you evaluate both speed and correctness?

lessons.title: Monitoring
lessons.description: Track latency, errors, cost, and safety events in one place.
checklist: I can define meaningful dashboards.
I can set alert thresholds.
I can act on incidents quickly.
questions: What metric mix catches most production issues?
Which alert should trigger immediate action?

curatedResources.title: AI Engineering
curatedResources.placement: Use while selecting production observability strategy.
curatedResources.why: Frames reliability tradeoffs and operation priorities in real systems.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use when choosing monitoring scope and incident priorities.

lessons.title: Cost Control
lessons.description: Optimize spend with caching, model choice, and routing logic.
checklist: I can set up token and request budgets.
I can choose cheaper models for simple tasks.
I can implement caching with cache invalidation.
questions: How do you prevent runaway inference spend?
When is caching highest-value?

lessons.title: Security
lessons.description: Protect secrets, input channels, and model outputs.
checklist: I can secure secrets and APIs.
I can sanitize inputs and outputs.
I can enforce least privilege for tools.
questions: What are common attack surfaces in AI APIs?
How do you prevent prompt and data leakage?

curatedResources.title: Building Effective Agents
curatedResources.placement: Use for composable governance and safety boundaries.
curatedResources.why: Shows practical safety-oriented control patterns in AI systems.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use for high-risk action controls and user-data safeguards.

curatedResources.title: MCP Practical Guide
curatedResources.placement: Use during secure tool and API connection design.
curatedResources.why: Focuses on safe external integration patterns.
curatedResources.whenToUse: Use before exposing any high-privilege operations.

lessons.title: Deployment
lessons.description: Publish and operate AI features with safe rollout practices.
checklist: I can define environment promotion flow.
I can run staged rollouts and rollback.
I can document incident response steps.
questions: How do you test before full rollout?
What is your rollback trigger?

lessons.title: Mini Project: Production AI Feature
lessons.description: Add queue-backed API workflow and basic monitoring plan.
checklist: I can build async job flow.
I can define alert thresholds.
I can create rollback and incident notes.
questions: How would monitoring shape your first sprint?
Where could failure costs be highest?

lessons.title: Portfolio Project: Production AI SaaS Feature
lessons.description: Ship a production-ready AI feature with architecture, observability, and security notes.
checklist: I can document an end-to-end architecture.
I can define operational runbooks.
I can set measurable reliability targets.
questions: How do you balance quality, cost, and reliability?
What security controls do you monitor weekly?

lessons.title: Production AI Quiz
lessons.description: Validate production readiness and operating discipline.
checklist: I can design architecture for reliability.
I can propose rollout and rollback rules.
I can measure cost and safety continuously.
questions: What are the first production controls you would implement?
How do cost and trust influence architecture?

quizQuestions.prompt: What should your first AI architecture decision prioritize and why?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: It should prioritize reliable orchestration boundaries between model calls, policy checks, and user-facing responses so failures can be isolated and recovered.

options.label: Reliable orchestration boundaries
options.explanation: It should prioritize reliable orchestration boundaries between model calls, policy checks, and user-facing responses so failures can be isolated and recovered.

options.label: Provider choice first
options.explanation: Architecture should start with user workflow, reliability boundaries, latency, cost, and risk.

options.label: Synchronous everything
options.explanation: Long or unreliable jobs often need queues for retries, backpressure, and better user experience.

options.label: Uptime-only monitoring
options.explanation: AI production also needs quality, latency, cost, safety, drift, and fallback monitoring.

quizQuestions.prompt: When would you prefer queues over synchronous endpoints?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Use queues when requests are long-running, non-deterministic in timing, or need retries and status tracking without blocking the user request thread.

options.label: Queued long-running retries
options.explanation: Use queues when requests are long-running, non-deterministic in timing, or need retries and status tracking without blocking the user request thread.

options.label: Synchronous everything
options.explanation: Long or unreliable jobs often need queues for retries, backpressure, and better user experience.

options.label: Uptime-only monitoring
options.explanation: AI production also needs quality, latency, cost, safety, drift, and fallback monitoring.

options.label: Cache every response
options.explanation: Caching can reduce cost, but stale or user-specific responses can harm trust.

quizQuestions.prompt: Which monitoring set is most critical for AI production reliability?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Track latency, error rate, output rejection rate, cost per request, and model fallback rates with alert thresholds tied to business impact.

options.label: Uptime-only monitoring
options.explanation: AI production also needs quality, latency, cost, safety, drift, and fallback monitoring.

options.label: Cache every response
options.explanation: Caching can reduce cost, but stale or user-specific responses can harm trust.

options.label: Quality, latency, cost, safety
options.explanation: Track latency, error rate, output rejection rate, cost per request, and model fallback rates with alert thresholds tied to business impact.

options.label: Prompts are not security
options.explanation: AI systems still handle data, tools, permissions, injection risks, and audit requirements.

quizQuestions.prompt: How can model routing and caching reduce cost without harming trust?
quizQuestions.correctAnswer: Route simple tasks to cheaper models, cache deterministic outputs, and use stricter fallback policies for expensive calls while preserving quality checks.

options.label: Cache every response
options.explanation: Caching can reduce cost, but stale or user-specific responses can harm trust.

options.label: Prompts are not security
options.explanation: AI systems still handle data, tools, permissions, injection risks, and audit requirements.

options.label: Routing, caching, prompt limits
options.explanation: Route simple tasks to cheaper models, cache deterministic outputs, and use stricter fallback policies for expensive calls while preserving quality checks.

options.label: Cheapest model always
options.explanation: Routing should balance quality, latency, risk, and cost for each task.
